user_message: Just got this from Sandra Taylor at Northwind Traders -- I need help figuring out territory planning. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Northwind Traders)

[00:02:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:07:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:09:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:19:00] Michelle (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:24:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-J trap (Proposes architecture pattern customer explicitly rejected in transcript); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-J failure mode (Proposes architecture pattern customer explicitly rejected in transcript)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-J -- Proposes architecture pattern customer explicitly rejected in transcript
